critical_issues.title: Schema example on line 13 of get_1985_yankees.txt contained invalid JSON
critical_issues.description: The original schema example used bare word 'integer' as a value placeholder, making it unparseable JSON. A strict model could reproduce the literal non-JSON token 'integer' in output.
critical_issues.resolution: Replaced with a concrete valid JSON example: {"players": [{"name": "Don Mattingly", "position": "First Base", "jersey_number": 23}]}

critical_issues.title: function_app.py and trapi_client.py compute divergent SHA-256 hashes for the same prompt file
critical_issues.description: function_app._prompt_hash() hashes raw file bytes while trapi_client._prompt_hash() hashes normalized text (trailing whitespace/newlines stripped). This produces different digests for the same file, making cross-component audit correlation impossible.
critical_issues.resolution: Noted as out-of-scope for APP-01 (requires src/validator.py to be implemented first). function_app.py should be updated in a follow-up task to use trapi_client._load_prompt() and _prompt_hash().
critical_issues.status: DEFERRED - pre-existing issue in function_app.py, beyond APP-01 scope

major_issues.title: test_sha256_is_stable was vacuous - only checked format, not pinned value
major_issues.description: Original test only asserted SHA-256 output had 64 hex chars (always true). Any prompt change would pass.
major_issues.resolution: Pinned expected normalized SHA-256 as constant EXPECTED_PROMPT_SHA256 and assert equality.

major_issues.title: Prompt delivered as role:user rather than role:system in trapi_client.py
major_issues.description: System-level instructions are sent as user message. GPT-4o is more reliably constrained by system messages for strict output format requirements.
major_issues.resolution: Pre-existing code in trapi_client.py, beyond APP-01 scope. Recommend addressing in a TRAPI integration task.
major_issues.status: DEFERRED - pre-existing issue in trapi_client.py, beyond APP-01 scope

minor_issues.title: test_requests_24_to_28_players matched incidental jersey number substrings
minor_issues.description: assert '24' in prompt_text and '28' in prompt_text passed due to jersey numbers, not the roster count range phrase.
minor_issues.resolution: Replaced with regex: re.search(r'24[–\-]28|24 to 28|between 24 and 28', prompt_text)

minor_issues.title: test_requests_active_roster_only used OR condition that always passed
minor_issues.description: 'active' in lower OR 'roster' in lower always passed since 'roster' appears throughout the prompt.
minor_issues.resolution: Changed to AND condition requiring both 'active' and 'roster' to be present.

minor_issues.title: No .gitattributes enforcing LF line endings for prompt files
minor_issues.description: Without enforced LF endings, Windows contributors could silently introduce CRLF, changing the SHA-256 hash.

new_test_added.title: Added test_schema_example_is_valid_json
new_test_added.description: New test parses the schema example line with json.loads() to prevent future regressions of the invalid-JSON issue.
